pattern: Sum one second per typed letter plus the cheaper of clockwise and counterclockwise rotation between consecutive letters on a 26-letter ring.
hints: The pointer starts on 'a'; between two letters you may rotate either way around the circle.
The cost to move between two letters is the smaller of the forward gap and 26 minus that gap.
Add one second to type each character and accumulate min(d, 26-d) for each step where d is the absolute alphabet distance from the previous letter.

edge_inputs.description: first example moving forward through abc
wrap-around is cheaper than forward from b to z
single character at the start position
single far character requires wrap-around choice
repeated character costs only typing after first arrival
exact half distance of 13 ties both directions

stress.description: long random lowercase words exercising both rotation directions
stress.note: random letters stress the min over wrap-around
